note: migration-plan is a disable-model-invocation skill — human-run by design; it must NEVER auto-trigger from prose. This dataset is negative-assurance only: every entry is a near-miss in the migration / upgrade / large-refactor neighborhood where the correct behavior is to route to a sibling skill (refactor-audit / refactor-smells / refactor-plan / refactor-apply, debug, explain, docs-search, task-start, adr, sec-deps, changelog) or to a plain git/shell action — or to wait for an explicit /marvin:migration-plan invocation by the human. No positives, no competition entries. mock_rate is 0 on every query so --decider mock stays green.

queries.text: run the pending Rails db migrations against my local dev database
queries.note: executing schema migrations is a plain shell/CLI action (rails db:migrate), not planning a migration

queries.text: explain how our current auth flow works before I think about replacing it with OAuth
queries.note: understanding existing code → explain, not producing a migration plan

queries.text: audit src/legacy/ for code smells and refactoring opportunities
queries.note: read-only code-health scan → refactor-smells / refactor-audit, not a phased migration plan

queries.text: the upgrade to React 19 broke our test suite — a bunch of useEffect hooks now fire twice, help me figure out why
queries.note: diagnosing breakage after an upgrade → debug, not planning the upgrade

queries.text: check package.json and requirements.txt for outdated deps with known CVEs before we upgrade
queries.note: vulnerability/version audit of manifests → sec-deps, not a migration plan

queries.text: actually carry out step 3 of the plan — rename the UserService module and update all the imports
queries.note: executing a planned refactor step → refactor-apply, not re-planning it

queries.text: where are our db schema migration conventions documented?
queries.note: finding existing docs → docs-search, not authoring a plan

queries.text: write an ADR capturing our decision to migrate from REST to gRPC
queries.note: recording the decision rationale → adr, not the step-by-step migration plan

queries.text: my manager asked me to spec out the task of splitting the monorepo — can you start the task and produce the spec?
queries.note: producing an immutable task spec via the pipeline → task-start; migration-plan is a separate human-run planning surface

queries.text: generate a rollback SQL script that drops the columns the 0042 migration added
queries.note: writing a concrete rollback script is a code-gen/shell task, not planning the migration; rollback strategy inside a plan is different

queries.text: walk me through what /marvin:migration-plan asks for before I run it on the Postgres-to-DynamoDB move
queries.note: asks ABOUT the plan surface; the plan itself is an explicit human /marvin:migration-plan run, never auto-loaded from prose

queries.text: summarize what changed in the framework upgrade between v2.3 and v3.0 for the release notes
queries.note: release-note summary from git history → changelog, not a forward migration plan

queries.text: grep the codebase for every call site of the deprecated fetchLegacy() so I know the blast radius
queries.note: a one-shot usage grep is a plain shell action; migration-plan does impact analysis as part of a full human-invoked plan, not on an ad-hoc grep request

queries.text: break down the smells report into a sequenced set of refactor steps I can execute
queries.note: turning a findings register into a step plan → refactor-plan (ADR-0029 rails), not the migration-plan surface
